metadata.title: Your Targeted Nanoparticle Doesn't Reach More Tumor. It Just Enters More Cells.
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Cancer Nanomedicine candidate 03. One claim: uptake does not equal accumulation, because the targeting ligand acts only at the last step.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on real newsprint scan; desaturated plates; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines; hand-drawn annotation ring used once per graphic at most
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, teal = particle accumulation in tumor / what's actually measured; crimson = cell uptake / the last-step-only action; gold = the binding step (editor's-pen highlight, fill only, never text)

metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = particle accumulation in tumor / what's actually measured; CRIMSON = cell uptake / the last-step-only action; GOLD = the binding step
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: protein corona (own video), EC145-vs-mirvetuximab case, the 0.7% figure, EPR's contested status. One claim only: uptake does not equal accumulation, because the ligand acts last.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Pragmatist register (voices/pragmatist/VOICE.md + HAI.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the HAI outro from AUTHOR.MD :: Humanitarians AI
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: A nanoparticle decorated with antibodies binds cancer cells ten times better in culture. In an animal, both the targeted and untargeted particles reach the tumor in nearly equal amounts.

card.copy: Your Targeted Nanoparticle Doesn't Reach More Tumor.

beats.narration_text: The antibody performs in culture. Tumor biodistribution data shows a different result: the targeting ligand produced almost no difference in how much drug reached the tumor.

props.headline: The antibody works beautifully in the lab. The tumor data tells…

beats.scene_description: two side-by-side petri dishes: left labeled 'Targeted' with densely clustered nanoparticles (teal dots) bound to cancer cells, right labeled 'Untargeted' with sparse binding; push-in on the 'Targeted' dish, then pull back to show both; editorial newsprint desaturated treatment
beats.image_prompt: B02, two petri dishes seen from above on an aged newsprint surface, left dish labeled 'TARGETED' filled with dense teal dots bound to pink cancer cells, right dish labeled 'UNTARGETED' with sparse teal dots, clean editorial diagram, desaturated, flat print reproduction
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] two dishes — targeted vs untargeted in culture

beats.narration_text: A ligand with ten-fold better cell binding in culture does not increase tumor accumulation in the animal. The ligand is functional. Why does it fail to improve delivery?

production_viz.label: gap formula card — the question
production_viz.mechanic: serif quote card fades in: 'The ligand works. Why doesn't it help?' with gold underline; teal chip 'ACCUMULATION' and crimson chip 'UPTAKE' bracket the question
production_viz.note: the gap formula — question on screen before answer

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] gap formula — 'The ligand works. Why doesn't it help?'

beats.narration_text: Delivery is a sequential chain: blood circulation, tumor vessel wall, tumor interstitium, then cell surface. The targeting ligand acts only at the final step — cell binding and internalization.

production_viz.label: delivery chain — four steps, ligand acts last
production_viz.mechanic: four horizontal steps draw on left to right: 'BLOOD' (teal bar) -> 'VESSEL WALL' (teal bar) -> 'TUMOR TISSUE' (teal bar) -> 'CELL SURFACE' (crimson bar, labeled 'ligand acts here'); a serif label 'ligand acts only here' annotates the final step
production_viz.note: chain progression shows the ligand's position in the sequence

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] delivery chain — ligand acts at step four only

beats.narration_text: Cell culture measures step four only — binding and entry — in conditions with no blood flow, no vessel walls, no immune clearance. Steps one through three are invisible to it.

production_viz.label: what cell culture can and cannot see
production_viz.mechanic: left column 'CELL CULTURE': one crimson bar labeled 'cell uptake (step 4)' visible; steps 1-3 are greyed out / absent. Right column 'IN THE ANIMAL': all four teal bars visible; an annotation ring highlights steps 1-3 as 'the part you haven't measured'
production_viz.note: the diagnostic gap — culture only sees step 4

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] culture sees one step; body has four

beats.narration_text: Tumor accumulation is determined at steps one and two: circulation half-life and vessel permeability. The targeting ligand has no effect on either. It cannot increase the amount of drug that reaches the tumor.

production_viz.label: what sets tumor accumulation
production_viz.mechanic: two teal arrows converge on a 'TUMOR' teal bucket: top arrow labeled 'circulation half-life', bottom arrow labeled 'vessel permeability'; a third crimson arrow from 'targeting ligand' points at the bucket but is barred (a cross-bar line) labeled 'no effect here'
production_viz.note: the mechanism — accumulation drivers precede the ligand

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] two teal arrows set accumulation; ligand barred

beats.narration_text: The ligand acts only after the particle has arrived at the tumor: it improves internalization at the cell surface. It cannot retroactively increase accumulation — that was determined upstream.

production_viz.label: the ligand's moment of action — after arrival
production_viz.mechanic: two teal particle dots arrive at a 'TUMOR TISSUE' box (equal, same size); then only the targeted one (crimson outline added) docks into a cell receptor with a gold binding flash; the untargeted stays in the interstitium; serif label 'same arrival, different uptake'
production_viz.note: the compare move — equal accumulation, different cellular entry

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] equal arrival; targeted particle enters cell

beats.narration_text: When accumulation is the bottleneck — too few particles reaching the tumor — improving cellular uptake does not help. The fix must match the failure mode. Fixing step four when steps one or two are the constraint wastes the intervention.

production_viz.label: fixing step 4 when the bottleneck is step 1 or 2
production_viz.mechanic: the delivery chain from B04 reappears; a CRIMSON bracket highlights step 4 labeled 'you improved this'; a TEAL bracket highlights steps 1-2 labeled 'the actual bottleneck'; a serif label 'wrong fix' in crimson annotates
production_viz.note: implication — the fix must match the bottleneck

beats.narration_text: Folate-targeted versus bare particles, equal dose. At four hours: tumor accumulation 2.1% versus 1.9% — nearly identical. Cellular internalization: 68% versus 12%. Same tumor-level drug. Entirely different cell-level entry.

production_viz.label: folate example — equal accumulation, different uptake (illustrative)
production_viz.mechanic: two columns side by side: 'TARGETED' and 'UNTARGETED'. Top row: teal accumulation bars same height, labeled '2.1%' and '1.9%' (ILLUSTRATIVE). Bottom row: crimson internalization bars very different heights, labeled '68%' and '12%' (ILLUSTRATIVE). A serif label 'illustrative numbers' in small text
production_viz.note: illustrative numbers per card — must be labeled ILLUSTRATIVE in FACTCHECK

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] folate example — two-column compare bar chart

beats.narration_text: Cell binding in culture is not tumor accumulation in vivo. The ligand acts at the final step. Accumulation is determined upstream by circulation half-life and vessel permeability — fix those first.

card.sub: The ligand acts last. Fix the early steps.

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] endcard — uptake does not equal accumulation

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: This is part of the Cancer Nanomedicine series from Humanitarians AI.

props.tagline: AI education for humanitarian and social-impact practitioners.
